items.title: Render purity and Strict Mode: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this React: Component-Based Browser Applications misconception? A fetch call during render runs once because React renders components only once.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Render can repeat or be discarded; keep side effects out of render and synchronize external systems in events or effects.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Render can repeat or be discarded; keep side effects out of render and synchronize external systems in events or effects.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked React: Component-Based Browser Applications correction rather than the documented misconception.

options.text: Render can repeat or be discarded; keep side effects out of render and synchronize external systems in events or effects.

items.title: Render purity and Strict Mode: verified implementation facet
items.prompt: Avoid side effects during render and use development double invocation to expose impurities. Build a bounded artifact demonstrating render without observable external side effect; the locked browser verifier checks observable structure, behavior, or computed presentation.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted authoring draft. Runtime evidence does not replace independent human standards, pedagogy, accessibility, answer-oracle, and formal-exam review.

hints: Implement the smallest artifact that satisfies the declared observable contract, then check the visible case before considering the hidden boundary.

feedback.correct: The locked authoring verifier observed the declared bounded facet in both visible and hidden cases.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic authoring check failed; inspect the exact artifact, interaction state, viewport, and observable result without assuming hidden-case details.

criteria.description: Passes the visible and hidden deterministic checks for the declared implementation facet in its pinned authoring environment.

privateAuthorNotes: This code item and its oracle are AI-assisted draft material with reviewer=null and examEligibility=false.
Browser-verifier items are authoring evidence only and must never be routed to the official untrusted-code runner or formal exam pipeline.

answer.explanation: The reference is a bounded react artifact for render without observable external side effect. Passing proves only the declared observable facet, not holistic mastery or human accessibility approval.
